element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns isometric perspective, track navigation with hills/dips, guardrail avoidance
Attack bonus system rewards destroying opponent vehicles; vehicles respawn quickly so failure is non-punitive

element_introduction_order.element: Track hazards (mines, oil slicks, snow drifts, lava)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Environmental hazards vary by planet; players learn to avoid or use strategically

element_introduction_order.element: Economic upgrade system (engines, tires, shocks, shields, weapons, turbo)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Money earned through attack bonuses and lapping bonuses; upgrades provide competitive advantage for later races

element_introduction_order.element: Points progression system (two divisions per planet)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Points required for advancement; failure to earn enough points forces division replay (but retains money/car)
Comic-book-inspired humans and aliens; purchasing new vehicles resets equipment upgrades

element_introduction_order.element: Multiplayer vs AI racing (up to two players)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Four racers per race: up to two players + Rip/Shred (constant AI) + one planet-unique opponent

typical_relief_triggers: Gaining full lap lead (lapping bonus reward)
Finishing race and entering upgrade menu
typical_tension_triggers: Collision with track hazards (mines, oil slicks, lava)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Continuous four-lap cycle with embedded tension/relief micro-cycles per lap; economic meta-game between races provides medium-term tension around point accumulation

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory difficulty on first planet; players learn isometric navigation and basic combat
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Point requirements escalate; hazard variety increases across planets; economic pressure for upgrades becomes relevant
notable_spikes: Division progression gates (insufficient points require replay)
Hazard-specific planets introduce new environmental challenges requiring tactical adaptation
Multiplayer competition (if engaged) increases unpredictability vs AI opponents

notable_level_patterns.description: Each planet contains two divisions of races; advancement requires points thresholds; failure repeats division from zero points but retains money/vehicle state
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Generic across all planets (named but not detailed in source)

notable_level_patterns.pattern_name: Four-lap race structure with persistent hazards
notable_level_patterns.description: All races consist of four laps; tracks feature isometric slopes, guardrails, and planet-specific hazards (mines, oil, snow, lava); weapon/boost charges refresh per lap completion

notable_level_patterns.pattern_name: Vehicular combat as primary race mechanic
notable_level_patterns.description: Heavy emphasis on attacking opponent vehicles for attack bonuses; vehicles respawn quickly; lapping bonus awarded for one-lap lead; money rewards fund economic progression
